commands.review_summary:
The PR adds trusted-tool-policy entries to the global hook-runner registry facade, passes that composed registry into before_tool_call policy diagnostics and evaluation, and adds regression tests for active/pinned registry drift.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +155. Total +220 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with high confidence. Current main composes live registries for hook dispatch while trusted-policy helpers read the active registry, and the related issue/merged PR history documents the live registry split.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is queued because the current head has no discrete fixable finding; remaining action is maintainer/security-boundary review.

target.title: fix: keep trusted policies with hook registry
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix `before_tool_call` trusted-policy lookup so it uses the same composed live plugin registry view as the global hook runner.
- Let trusted-policy helpers read an explicit registry while preserving the active-registry fallback for callers without a global hook registry.
- Add regression coverage for both active-registry drift and later global-runner reinitialization with pinned Gateway registries.

## What Changed
A Gateway run can keep plugin hooks live through the global hook runner while a later scoped/default registry becomes the active registry. Current `main` composes hook dispatch from the latest initialized registry plus live active/pinned registries. Before this change, trusted policies were still read from a single active/latest registry path, so a hook from a pinned Gateway registry could run while that registry's trusted policy was invisible.

The fixed path exposes the same composed live registry view used by global hook dispatch and passes that registry into trusted-policy diagnostics and evaluation.

## Behavior Addressed
Tool calls that can still reach plugin `before_tool_call` hooks now evaluate trusted tool policies from the same composed live registry before the ordinary hook path runs.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: split-registry trusted-policy bypass in `runBeforeToolCallHook(...)`, including the current composed global hook-runner path.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on current `origin/main`, branch `jesse/fix-trusted-policy-registry`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts: 1 file passed, 13 tests passed
src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts: 1 file passed, 3 tests passed
src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts: 1 file passed, 62 tests passed
core tsgo: passed
source-test tsgo: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Observed result after fix: the new before-tool-call tests create a Gateway registry with a trusted policy, replace/reinitialize later registries, and confirm the trusted policy still blocks before the ordinary hook runs. The hook-runner-global test also verifies the composed registry exposes the pinned Gateway trusted policy after later initialization.

What was not tested: a rebuilt ASSP/RovoClaw container using this patched OpenClaw commit was not run after the code change. The earlier local ASSP debug run reproduced the callback-yes/policy-no symptom before this OpenClaw patch.

## Verification
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/hook-runner-global.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
passed

git diff --check
passed
```
